content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Our features collide on the same 3 make_context() calls in _resolve_context. Proposal: you own shell_completion.py entirely (your with-statement wrapping), I own core.py entirely (Context.validate_nesting logic - __enter__/__exit__/close already exist, I'll extend close()). Since you're already touching those 3 make_context() call sites for the with-wrapping, please also add validate_nesting=False as a kwarg to each of those 3 calls (cli.make_context(...) and the two cmd.make_context(...) calls) so shell completion opts out of my new validation. That's the only thing I need in shell_completion.py. OK with this split?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Accepted: I own core.py, you own shell_completion.py. Please avoid the resilient_parsing shortcut - my task spec explicitly requires shell_completion.py to set validate_nesting=False explicitly, not infer it from resilient_parsing. Exact instructions for _resolve_context (around line 546-582 currently): (1) right after 'ctx_args["resilient_parsing"] = True', add 'ctx_args["validate_nesting"] = False' so it flows into cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), **ctx_args); (2) in 'ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True)' add ', validate_nesting=False'; (3) in the sub_ctx = cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True,) call, add 'validate_nesting=False,' too. Context.make_context/make_context already just forwards extra kwargs to Context(), and validate_nesting will default to False anyway so this is purely explicit/defensive - should not conflict with your with-statement wrapping. Thanks! Sending DONE from my side once core.py is done.
